field.label: Enable ShadowSocks Server
field.help: This will activate the ShadowSocks Server. This lets people around the world connect via ss-local to your server.

field.label: Server Address
field.help: IP address or hostname of the server.

field.label: Local Port
field.help: The local port of the daemon, default is fine.

field.label: Password
field.help: Password to authenticate against the server.

field.label: Cipher
field.help: Choose the cipher to use for encryption.

field.label: Mode
field.help: Choose TCP, UDP or both relay mode

field.label: Timeout
field.help: Set the TCP relay timeout in seconds.

field.label: Fragmentation
field.help: Allow IP fragmentation on the outbound UDP socket.

field.label: Enable outbound destination blocklist
field.help: Generate a Shadowsocks server ACL using [outbound_block_list]. This blocks destinations after sslocal has proxied traffic into this server. Add only the destinations you want to block below.

field.label: Blocked IPv4 destinations
field.help: Comma separated IPv4 addresses or CIDR networks to block, for example 172.28.0.0/16,10.1.0.0/16,10.152.183.0/24.

field.label: Blocked IPv6 destinations
field.help: Comma separated IPv6 addresses or CIDR networks to block, for example fd01::/108,fd98::/108,2a02:8010:6538::/48.
